updates.tweet_text: @Marcel22520116 It has to be a major shelf of support that breaks down to produce a large selloff. ITs very specific, has to be a multi-day very clear shelf with numerous tests that then collapses through. Can't just be any random support
updates.notes: Reply explaining a conceptual rule about shelf breakdowns; no specific price named or actionable level introduced.

updates.tweet_text: Closing update #ES_F: Today's final target was 7597. Targets given at 8am were 7547, 7565, 7577, then 7597. We hit ~7597, and rejected. Now, we will rebuild structure and then prep for breakout. We are building a 1.5 week flag which is 60% mature. Newsletter out soon with entries
updates.notes: All prices mentioned (7547, 7565, 7577, 7597) are morning-plan levels or hindsight references to targets already given at 8am. No new actionable levels named.

updates.tweet_text: @paigeclay0 Failed Breakdowns work best if the wick of the failed breakdown can also tag a big support. Then the failed breakdown is backed up by a direct support test
updates.notes: Reply tweet explaining FBD mechanics conceptually; no price named, no actionable level introduced.

updates.tweet_text:

updates.tweet_text:
We are working the targets today and as I've warned all week, don't make summer trading hard. Markets go *elevator down*, then they rally for days. 7482 reclaim was last entry Wednesday. My target was 7597 (hit). Today's slate: 7597 again (hit), 7611, 7624.

No support until 55 https://t.co/yDIjbvM630
updates.notes: 7597/7611/7624 are morning-plan or hindsight targets; 7482 is existing; '55' is incomplete/ambiguous. No new actionable levels named.

updates.tweet_text:
Micro dip there in #ES_F but levels are ultra precise today. As I tweeted 11mins ago, the first support today is 7555. We sold to 7555 almost exact, and bounced 30 points. Summer trading is 95% upward grind, mixed with brief "elevator down" dips

7597 (hit), 7611, 7624 upsides https://t.co/rFPMr9ouLX
updates.notes: 7611 and 7624 are new upside targets not in the morning plan; 7555 and 7597 are morning-plan reaffirmations.

updates.tweet_text: @Trevormiles16 Only 51 points. If you see larger it may be a platform error

updates.tweet_text: @bhargaval All elevator down sells are headline driven (they don't just happen randomly). I basically always hold a runner over the weekend though as long as my cost basis is low enough. All war dips will be bought without exception
updates.notes: Pure conversational reply about trade philosophy and holding runners over weekend. No actionable price levels named.

updates.tweet_text: @ewtnewbie You just buy it! Trading involves risk. As I said in the newsletter I don't take those trades (I only take Faild Breakdowns) but I give the levels and entries out and people can engage how they want. In this case we did flush it and recover though, so a little safer
updates.notes: Reply explaining trading philosophy and trade management (FBD preference). No new actionable price levels named.

updates.tweet_text:
Nothing to do but close platform. As I've said all week, summer #ES_F trading is easy. 1) Wait for the daily "elevator down sell" 2) Long the Failed Breakdowns (I do not short, ever) 3) Ride runner for 1-3 days until repeat

7555 was today's support, held. 7624 next, then 7695+ https://t.co/71R4Ayqwwo
updates.notes: 7624 and 7695 named as sequential upside targets ('next, then') not in morning plan; 7555 is a morning plan level (reaffirmation only).

updates.tweet_text: @ChadWinward It was buyable. Could've entered on the 70 low recovery, or on the 55 sweep, or just bought the 55. I didn't trade it as I only take high quality Failed Breakdowns that have a nearly 100% win rate (this was much lower) but it was there for those who wanted it.
